Title: A Maize Gene Regulatory Network for Phenolic Metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acid substrates such as 4-coumarate to their CoA thioesters, producing 4-coumaroyl-CoA for entry into downstream phenolic branches.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-centered Y1H; integration with ChIP-seq; network analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Identified as a key early phenylpropanoid pathway gene whose promoter is extensively targeted by multiple transcription factors in the maize phenolic regulatory network.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant-environment interaction; structural and specialized phenolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native 4CL3 promoter fragment (~1 kb upstream region) used as Y1H bait; p4CL3::Luc reporter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Protoplast assays in etiolated seedling leaves; dark incubation for transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for TSS definition; ChIP-seq and RNA-seq information integrated from published datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 was selected as one of 54 core phenolic biosynthetic gene promoters used to construct the maize phenolic GRN. 2. It represents an early step in the general phenylpropanoid pathway and is controlled by many TFs in the integrated network. 3. A 4CL3-centered subnetwork revealed multiple feed-forward loops involving KN1, P1, FEA4, and secondary TFs.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulatory node for engineering flux into multiple downstream phenolic branches, but broad pathway position suggests pleiotropic effects should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on promoter regulation rather than direct enzyme functional assays for 4CL3 itself.

Pathway_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates caffeic acid or related lignin-pathway intermediates to form feruloyl-type products contributing to monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts or determines lignin branch composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter-centered Y1H; ChIP-qPCR validation for selected TF-promoter interactions; integration with published ChIP-seq
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COMT1 is a heavily targeted lignin-pathway gene promoter in the maize phenolic GRN and is highlighted as controlling the conversion between G- and S-lignin.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support and environmental interaction via lignin/phenolic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native COMT1 promoter fragment used as Y1H bait
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Protoplast-based ChIP validation in etiolated seedling leaves
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for TSS definition; ChIP-seq and RNA-seq integrated from published datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT1 was one of the most connected promoters in the network, recognized by 161 TFs. 2. It is a late lignin-branch gene important for G- to S-lignin conversion. 3. COMT1-centered subnetworks revealed multiple feed-forward loops and included both activators and repressors.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for altering lignin composition in maize; however, extensive upstream regulation indicates engineering should account for network complexity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: COMT1 corresponds to brown midrib3 (bm3) and was screened more deeply than some other promoters, which may partly explain its high incoming connectivity.

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings used for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9 and additional phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PAL4; broadly binds and likely activates multiple phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; transient transactivation assay in maize protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator of maize phenolic pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major multi-target activator candidate for phenylpropanoid metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in feed-forward loop architecture within integrated GRN
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in maize protoplasts from etiolated B73 × Mo17 F1 seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for promoter definition; ChIP-qPCR; integrated ChIP-seq/RNA-seq from public maize studies
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB65 recognized 13 maize phenolic gene regulatory sequences, including seven PAL promoters. 2. ChIP-qPCR validated 10 of these 13 Y1H interactions in maize protoplasts. 3. MYB65 strongly activated a PAL4::Luc reporter, demonstrating transcriptional activator function in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for coordinated enhancement of phenylpropanoid flux, but broad target range may cause pleiotropic remodeling of multiple branches.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad metabolic and developmental effects due to multi-promoter control were not directly tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the maize ortholog of Arabidopsis AtMYB58/AtMYB63, known positive regulators of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding to phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds HCT6 and COMT1 in Y1H and ChIP; associates with 4CL3 by ChIP
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate regulator of lignin and general phenylpropanoid genes via promoter binding.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally supported network regulator, but not functionally established as master regulator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible indirect promoter recruitment in vivo for some targets
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Maize protoplast ChIP in etiolated seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB100 was used as an example to validate Y1H-discovered interactions in vivo. 2. ChIP confirmed interaction with HCT6 and COMT1, and additionally detected 4CL3 binding not found by Y1H. 3. The results illustrated the complementarity of Y1H and ChIP for building the maize phenolic GRN.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory engineering target, but direct metabolic consequences were not established.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used primarily as a validation example rather than a fully characterized metabolic regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coordinated expression across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters in both phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-connectivity candidate regulator of maize phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: One of the strongest hub regulators in the network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD35 recognized 24 phenolic gene promoters, the highest outgoing connectivity detected. 2. Targets span both phenylpropanoid and flavonoid branches. 3. It was proposed as a candidate coordinator of pathway gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential high-impact regulator for broad phenolic remodeling, though function and pleiotropy require testing.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown because physiological validation was not reported.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD35 and close orthologs were noted as unknown.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coordinated expression across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters in both phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-connectivity candidate regulator of maize phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD24 recognized 23 phenolic gene promoters. 2. Its targets span multiple phenolic branches. 3. It was highlighted among the 11 TFs binding 10 or more promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for coordinated metabolic engineering of phenolic traits.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous to Arabidopsis LBD16, a transcriptional activator in lateral root formation, but maize phenolic role was newly suggested here.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Coordinated expression of phenolic pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters in phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Hub-like candidate regulator of maize phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD20 bound 18 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It was among the top 11 highly connected TFs in the phenolic GRN. 3. Its biological role in phenolic metabolism was previously unknown.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential broad-spectrum regulator for phenolic engineering, pending functional validation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD20 or its orthologs were described as unknown.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid pathway gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters across phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad candidate regulator across multiple phenolic branches.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hub regulator candidate linking multiple phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: p35S::GFP-DOF28 used for ChIP validation of selected interactions
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF28 recognized 15 promoters spanning phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches. 2. Selected Y1H-discovered interactions were corroborated by ChIP. 3. It was highlighted as one of the 11 major high-connectivity TFs.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for multi-branch phenolic trait manipulation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DOF family members have diverse metabolic and developmental roles; direct metabolic output changes were not measured here.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Regulation across all branches of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters across all phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate broad regulator of phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity regulator candidate in an understudied TF family
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPP8 bound 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway. 2. This implicated CPP family members in phenolic regulation. 3. CPP8 was among the 11 TFs recognizing 10 or more promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a new regulatory entry point for phenolic engineering from an otherwise poorly characterized TF family.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPP TFs were noted as significantly understudied in plants.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenolic biosynthesis with possible integration of nitrogen status
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes promoters corresponding to all branches of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggests a regulatory link between nitrogen status and phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen/cellular nitrate status inferred from NLP family function
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen status context inferred from NLP biology
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP9 recognized 13 promoters spanning all branches of phenolic biosynthesis. 2. The finding suggested a previously unrecognized connection between phenolic biosynthesis and nitrogen cellular status. 3. NLP9 was among the most connected TFs in the network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for coupling nutrient signaling with phenolic accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible nutrient-growth tradeoffs were not tested.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The text contains a likely typo in one place listing NLP14 among the top TFs, but the detailed results describe NLP9 as the 13-promoter binder.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Recognizes regulatory regions of genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate broad regulator of phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP63 recognized the regulatory regions of 12 genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway. 2. It was one of the most connected TFs discovered. 3. The study implicated bZIP-mediated control in phenolic pathway coordination.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulator for broad rewiring of phenolic biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The abstracted 11 top TFs list refers to bZIP13, but the detailed result section states bZIP63; the extraction follows the detailed results.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: General phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid gene regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL9; 4CL3; ALDH1; ALDH5; COMT1; F5H1; HCT6; HCT10; FLS1; C2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoters from three pathway sectors
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate integrator of multiple phenolic branches.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Hub regulator candidate connecting lignin and flavonoid modules
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H42 bound 10 promoters across general phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid branches. 2. Its targets included PAL9, 4CL3, COMT1, HCT genes, and flavonoid genes. 3. This implicated CCCH zinc finger TFs in broad phenolic regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for cross-branch phenolic engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family members are often involved in stress and developmental regulation, suggesting possible pleiotropy.

Regulation_Genes.Variety: B73; B73 × Mo17 F1 hybrid seedlings for protoplast assays
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz2; PAL5 and additional phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a phenolic gene promoter-binding regulator/co-regulator in maize.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Potential bridging interaction with TBP inferred from family biology
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Family known as transcriptional co-activators bridging TBP and specific TFs; direct promoter recognition also discussed
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBF1.3 recognized 10 phenolic biosynthesis gene promoters in Y1H. 2. Two interactions, with Bz2 and PAL5 promoters, were validated by ChIP in maize protoplasts. 3. The study provided evidence that MBF1 family members can directly participate in phenolic gene regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting candidate for tuning pathway expression through co-regulatory mechanisms, but mode of action remains incompletely resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper discusses that MBF1 proteins blur the TF versus co-regulator distinction.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Works with NAC37 to activate Bz1; can activate A1 alone and NAC37 can impair A1 activation by LBD4
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls anthocyanin gene promoters through promoter-specific combinatorial regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Illustrative regulator for combinatorial control of anthocyanin genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms a functional interacting TF pair with NAC37
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD4 and NAC37 were together required to activate the Bz1 promoter in yeast. 2. LBD4 alone could activate the A1 promoter, and NAC37 reduced this activation. 3. Y2H demonstrated physical interaction between LBD4 and NAC37.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights promoter-specific TF-combination logic that should be considered when engineering anthocyanin traits.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter-dependent antagonism/cooperation may complicate engineering outcomes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as an example of combinatorial gene regulation rather than a globally connected hub.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Bz1; A1 (modulatory effect in combination with LBD4)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required with LBD4 for Bz1 activation; impairs LBD4-mediated activation of A1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts with LBD4 in promoter-specific combinatorial regulation of anthocyanin genes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Example of combinatorial controller rather than standalone master regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC37 functioned together with LBD4 to activate the Bz1 promoter in yeast. 2. NAC37 physically interacted with LBD4 in Y2H. 3. On the A1 promoter, NAC37 reduced the activation mediated by LBD4 alone.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates that anthocyanin engineering may require managing TF combinations rather than single regulators.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promoter-specific inhibitory effects may create nonintuitive outcomes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The in planta role in maize anthocyanin accumulation remains to be determined according to the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Requires EREB12 for Bz1 promoter activation in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts cooperatively with EREB12 to activate the Bz1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZHD14 alone was insufficient to activate Bz1 in yeast. 2. Co-expression with EREB12 enabled Bz1 promoter activation. 3. ZHD14 physically interacted with EREB12 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests combinatorial TF design strategies may be needed for pathway activation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene manipulation may fail without partner TF context.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as an example of TF cooperation revealed uniquely by the transformation-based Y1H strategy.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Works cooperatively with ZHD14 to activate Bz1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed Y1H cooperation assay; Y2H physical interaction assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts cooperatively with ZHD14 to activate the Bz1 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EREB12 required co-expression with ZHD14 to activate Bz1 in yeast. 2. Physical interaction with ZHD14 was verified by Y2H. 3. The pair exemplifies combinatorial control within the phenolic GRN.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reveals potential need for stacked regulatory manipulation to modify anthocyanin traits.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Context dependence may limit predictive engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a mechanistic example of TF cooperation.
